name: Miami
tagline: Beach Life, Latin Culture, and Zero State Income Tax
description: Miami is a sun-drenched, cosmopolitan city where Latin American flair meets American ambition on the shores of Biscayne Bay. With no state income tax, year-round tropical weather, and one of the most dynamic food scenes in the country, Miami offers FIRE retirees a genuinely international lifestyle without leaving the United States. Housing costs are high and rising fast, especially in waterfront neighborhoods, but the trade-off is direct access to world-class beaches, a thriving arts district in Wynwood, and a multicultural energy that makes every day feel like a vacation.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for US citizens
quickFacts.climate: Tropical with hot, humid summers and warm, dry winters

housing.description: 1BR apartment in Hialeah, Kendall, or outer Little Havana

dining.description: Cuban ventanitas and food trucks 2-3x/week; mostly cooking at home

healthcare.description: ACA marketplace Silver plan; Florida premiums can be steep without subsidies

transportation.description: Paid-off car with basic insurance and gas; a car is essential outside downtown

entertainment.description: Free beach days at South Beach and Key Biscayne, Wynwood street art walks, public parks

1M.lifestyle: A modest apartment in Hialeah or Kendall, well outside the beach and Brickell areas. You need a car, and housing costs eat up nearly half the budget. Cuban ventanitas and food trucks keep dining cheap, and the beach is free. Florida's zero state income tax is a real advantage at this budget level -- every dollar of your $3,333 monthly withdrawal stays with you. Summer A/C bills are a significant expense, but you never pay for heat.

housing.description: 2BR apartment in Coral Gables, Coconut Grove, or a condo in Edgewater

dining.description: Regular dining in Wynwood and Brickell 3-4x/week; weekend brunch at waterfront cafes

healthcare.description: ACA Gold plan with dental and vision; access to Baptist Health and Jackson Memorial

transportation.description: Reliable car with full coverage; Metrorail for downtown trips

entertainment.description: Heat games occasionally, Perez Art Museum, gym membership, salsa nights in Little Havana

2M.lifestyle: You can afford a comfortable apartment in Coral Gables, Coconut Grove, or Edgewater. You eat out several times a week in Wynwood and Brickell, keep a reliable car, and have room in the budget for Heat games and weekend drives to the Keys. No state income tax means the full $6,667 monthly budget is available to spend. The city's Latin-influenced dining and nightlife scene is accessible without stretching.

housing.description: 3BR home in Coconut Grove, waterfront condo in Brickell, or a house in Coral Gables

dining.description: Fine dining at Mandolin, Ariete, and KYU; regular date nights along the waterfront

healthcare.description: Premium private health insurance with comprehensive coverage and low deductibles

transportation.description: Late-model SUV or sedan, premium insurance, occasional Uber Black for nights out

entertainment.description: Heat season tickets, Art Basel events, boat rental days on Biscayne Bay, premium gym

3M.lifestyle: A nice house in Coconut Grove or a waterfront condo in Brickell. You dine at good restaurants regularly, drive a late-model car, and have budget for boat rental days on Biscayne Bay and weekend trips to the Keys. At $10,000 a month with no state income tax, Miami is genuinely comfortable. Art Basel events, Heat season tickets, and a premium gym membership all fit within the budget.

housing.description: Luxury waterfront condo in Brickell Key, a home on Key Biscayne, or a Coral Gables estate

dining.description: Private chef for entertaining, fine dining at Zuma, Komodo, and Juvia multiple nights per week

healthcare.description: Concierge medicine at Baptist Health, executive health screenings, premium private insurance

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle, boat slip at a Biscayne Bay marina, occasional private car service

entertainment.description: Courtside Heat tickets, VIP Art Basel access, yacht days, country club membership, deep-sea fishing

domesticHelp.description: Weekly house cleaning, pool and landscaping service, hurricane preparation service

luxuryExtras.description: Quarterly Caribbean travel, boat ownership costs, spa retreats, art collecting

5M.lifestyle: A luxury waterfront condo on Brickell Key or a house on Key Biscayne. You have a housekeeper, a nice car, and a boat slip at a Biscayne Bay marina. Dining at top restaurants is routine, and business class flights to the Caribbean or Latin America are easy weekend additions. Florida's zero income tax is a major advantage at this budget -- the full $16,667 monthly withdrawal goes toward lifestyle rather than state taxes.

housing.description: Star Island estate, Fisher Island residence, or penthouse in a luxury Brickell tower

dining.description: Full-time personal chef, catered events, exclusive restaurant experiences, private wine dinners

healthcare.description: Top concierge medical practice, executive wellness programs, specialist access at UHealth

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, yacht with captain, private car service, chartered flights from Opa-locka

entertainment.description: Luxury box at Hard Rock Stadium, major arts philanthropy, international travel, yacht cruises to the Bahamas

domesticHelp.description: Full-time estate manager, housekeeper, personal assistant, boat captain, pool and grounds crew

luxuryExtras.description: Yacht ownership, first-class international travel, real estate investing, philanthropic giving, art collecting

10M.lifestyle: A large residence on Fisher Island or a penthouse in a luxury Brickell tower. You have a full-time housekeeper, a cook who comes in several times a week, and a personal assistant. You dine wherever you want, travel first class, and own a boat for day trips on Biscayne Bay. Florida's zero state income tax means the full $33,333 monthly budget is available for spending. The lifestyle is wealthy and comfortable, with year-round warm weather and easy access to the Caribbean.
